plan_metadata.name: DA Typespec Oauth No Reference Id

steps.description: Click the "Declarative Agent" option in the "New Project" dropdown within the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit extension in Visual Studio Code.

Click the "Start with TypeSpec for Microsoft 365 Copilot" option in the dropdown menu labeled "Add an action to your declarative agent" within the "Create Declarative Agent" dialog in Visual Studio Code.
Click the "Default folder" option in the folder selection dropdown within the Workspace Folder prompt in Visual Studio Code (Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit interface).

parameters.sample:
=== Generated Script ===
Language: bash

```bash
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -e
TARGET_FILE="/home/vscode/AgentsToolkitProjects/vscuse_app_hm6tg/src/agent/main.tsp"
URL="https://raw.githubusercontent.com/KennethBWSong/typeSpecSchema/main/github-agent.tsp"

# Ensure the destination file exists
if [ ! -f "$TARGET_FILE" ]; then
  echo "Error: Target file '$TARGET_FILE' does not exist." >&2
  exit 1
fi

# Download the file and overwrite the target
curl --fail --silent --show-error --max-time 60 "$URL" -o "$TARGET_FILE"
echo "File replaced successfully."
```

=== Execution Results ===
Exit Code: 0

=== STDOUT ===
File replaced successfully.

✓ Script executed successfully

steps.description: @code replace all the content in file: "/home/vscode/AgentsToolkitProjects/${{var:app_name}}/src/agent/main.tsp" with online code: "https://github.com/KennethBWSong/typeSpecSchema/blob/main/github-agent.tsp"
Click the "src" folder in the Explorer panel of Visual Studio Code to expand its contents.
Click the "main.tsp" file under the "src/agent" folder in the Explorer panel of Visual Studio Code to open the file.
Click the "Install" button in the TypeSpec extension recommendation popup at the bottom right of the Visual Studio Code window for main.tsp.
Press Ctrl+F to open the search bar in Visual Studio Code while editing the file main.tsp.
Type the #text:'model oauth' into the search field of the Find dialog in Visual Studio Code, located at the top of the editor window.
Click the highlighted "model oauth" text in line 354 of main.tsp within the Visual Studio Code editor, inside the src/agent directory.

Type the text '@authReferenceId("NzJmOTg4YmYtODZmMS00MWFmLTkxYWItMmQ3Y2QwMTFkYjQ3IyMzODM5YjI5YS0yYzYxLTQzZGEtYTA2Ni1lYThiMDkwM2RkNDE=")' into the main.tsp file at line 353 in Visual Studio Code, within the src/agent directory.
Save the "main.tsp" file in Visual Studio Code by pressing Ctrl+S while the file is open and active in the editor.
Click the "Provision" button under the "LIFECYCLE" section in the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit pane within Visual Studio Code.
Click the "Install" button in the extension recommendation popup for TypeSpec at the bottom-right of the Visual Studio Code window.
Click the red plus (+) button inside the "Message Copilot" input box to start composing a new message in the chat within the M365 Copilot web application interface.
Type #text:'list repos' into the main chat input box in the  conversation within the M365 Copilot web interface.
Click the "Always allow" button in the permission prompt within the M365 Copilot web chat interface.
Click the "Sign in to github agent" button in the chat panel within the M365 Copilot web interface.
